summary: Shift to a responsive, front-end-only Daily Planner Calendar website built with semantic HTML, CSS (Grid/Flexbox, variables), and vanilla JavaScript, using LocalStorage for per-date tasks/notes (no authenticated backend).
architectural_notes: Front-end-only implementation using vanilla JS with LocalStorage persistence (no authenticated per-user backend).
Login page should remain front-end-only (no real authentication/backend); treat as a UI gate/landing screen unless requirements change.
known_issues: Prior deployment attempt failed; nothing is live yet (deployment snag).

existing_features.title: Create a calendar UI that lets a signed-in user navigate months, select a day, and view whether that day has an existing note.
Add day detail editing for daily plan notes: create a new note for the selected day, edit existing note fields, and delete a note, with clear loading/success/error states.
Add a notes list view with basic filtering that helps users find entries (by date range and simple text search across title/body).
Establish a coherent visual theme and layout for the app (typography, spacing, component styling) and apply it consistently across calendar, day detail, and list views.

Make the app usable as a front-end-only daily planner by persisting all calendar/day-plan data in browser LocalStorage (no backend dependency required for creating, editing, or deleting tasks/notes). Data must automatically load on app start and automatically save after each change.

Provide a monthly calendar view that is generated dynamically in the UI, supports navigating between months, highlights today’s date, and visually indicates days that have saved tasks/notes in LocalStorage.

Implement a daily planning view that opens when a user selects a date from the calendar, and supports adding, editing, and deleting tasks/notes organized into three time-of-day sections: Morning, Afternoon, Evening.

Apply a clean, modern, soft pastel UI theme using CSS variables, with rounded cards/buttons and responsive layout using CSS Grid and Flexbox. Use semantic HTML elements for structure and keep all user-facing text in English.

Add UI/UX interactions and animations: smooth transition between calendar and daily views, hover effects on calendar dates, slide-in animation when adding tasks, and fade-out animation when deleting tasks. Include simple navigation with clear icons for moving between calendar and daily view.
Add a dedicated login screen that gates access to the planner UI using the existing Internet Identity context, and only render the calendar/daily planner once the user has a non-anonymous identity.
Provide an in-app logout control in the authenticated planner UI header that clears the current Internet Identity session using the existing identity hook and returns the user to the login screen.
Add more UI animations beyond the current set, focusing on additional micro-interactions across calendar and daily planning views while respecting prefers-reduced-motion.
Add a simple Profile page that is accessible after successful Internet Identity login, and provide a clear navigation control to open it from the authenticated app header.
Update the global color styles and background styling to create a refreshed, cohesive theme applied consistently across the login screen, header, calendar view, daily view, and footer.
Recreate/redesign the sign-in (login) page UI while keeping the existing Internet Identity login logic, loading state, and error handling behavior intact.

Update the Login screen UI to visually match the uploaded “glassmorphism login form” reference by applying a translucent, blurred (backdrop-filter) card treatment, subtle light borders, and a stronger background visual layer, while keeping the existing Internet Identity login logic, loading state, and error handling behavior intact.

Add more informational content to the Login screen to help first-time users, including: (1) a short “How it works” section explaining Internet Identity at a high level (no passwords), (2) a “Data & privacy” section clarifying that planner data is stored locally in the browser, and (3) a small “Getting started” checklist (e.g., sign in, pick a date, add tasks) placed on the login page without cluttering the primary call-to-action.

Add a generated scenic background image suitable for a glassmorphism login layout and render it as a static asset on the Login screen (served from frontend/public/assets/generated) with appropriate overlays to preserve text contrast.

Convert the existing authenticated "Profile" page into a "Settings" area that still shows the Internet Identity principal and local-storage privacy information, but is organized as a settings hub with clearly labeled sections and room for additional options.

Add user-configurable settings controls within the new Settings area and persist those preferences in browser LocalStorage, including: (1) a theme preference control (System/Light/Dark), (2) a reduced-motion preference control (System/On/Off) that complements existing prefers-reduced-motion handling, and (3) planner data management actions (Export planner data as a JSON download, Import planner data from a JSON file with validation, and Clear all planner data with a confirmation step).

Update authenticated navigation to treat "Settings" as a first-class destination: keep the existing header entry point but rename it from "Profile" to "Settings" and ensure the back button behavior remains correct when returning to the planner.

Enhance page/view transition animations between Calendar, Daily, and Settings views so that navigation feels more responsive and polished (e.g., subtle slide + fade), while keeping the existing navigation logic intact.

Add additional micro-interactions to the Monthly Calendar UI to improve tactile feedback (e.g., press states, focus-visible styling consistency, and a subtle selection feedback when a day is clicked), without changing calendar functionality.

Add additional micro-interactions to the Daily Plan task workflow (add/edit/delete) beyond the existing enter/delete animations, focusing on clarity and responsiveness (e.g., input focus transitions, inline edit open/close motion, and button feedback), without changing CRUD behavior or stored data.

Add subtle micro-interactions to the Settings screen controls (theme selector, motion selector, import/export/clear actions) to improve perceived quality (e.g., section entrance timing consistency, button/selector feedback), while keeping all existing logic and text unchanged.

Update the Sign-in (Login) screen background to use the user-uploaded image (image-1.png) as the scenic background layer behind the glassmorphism UI, while keeping the existing Internet Identity login behavior, loading state, error handling, and existing contrast overlays intact.

Add support for per-month background images by creating a month-to-image mapping and using it to select the correct background image based on the currently active month in the UI (Calendar view: displayed month; Daily view: selectedDate month; Settings view: current date month). If a month image is missing/unavailable, fall back to the existing /assets/image-1.png background.

Render the month-specific background images as static frontend assets (served from frontend/public/assets/generated) and integrate them into the existing background layer approach used on the Login screen (background image + contrast overlays), ensuring text contrast remains readable in both light and dark themes.
Add a modern sans-serif web font for the app and apply it globally so all text uses the new font consistently across Login, Calendar, Daily, and Settings views.

Increase overall text brightness/legibility by adjusting the global color tokens used for text (foreground, muted-foreground, and card-foreground) and ensuring readable contrast on glassmorphism cards and over background images in both light and dark themes.
Improve perceived text clarity by adding global typographic rendering enhancements (e.g., font smoothing and consistent letter rendering) without changing any existing app behavior or animations.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a calendar app that supports daily plan/notes entries
Add daily planning view for a selected date with task/notes CRUD, organized into Morning/Afternoon/Evening sections.
Implement LocalStorage-based persistence keyed by date for tasks/notes (save and load automatically).
Apply responsive modern UI/UX: soft pastel theme, rounded cards/buttons, clear icon navigation, highlight today’s date.
Add UI animations: smooth transitions between calendar and daily view, hover effects on dates, slide-in on task add, fade-out on delete.
Add a login page/screen (front-end only) to gate access to the planner UI; no authenticated backend implied.
Expand UI animations beyond current set (additional micro-interactions/transitions across views and controls).
Add a simple profile page to the app (accessible after login).
Update the app color styles, including adding/updating background styling (refresh visual theme).
Recreate/redesign the sign-in (login) page UI while keeping it front-end-only (no real authentication backend).
Enhance the login page by adding more informational content (e.g., guidance/help text or app info) as needed.
Expand the user Profile page into a Settings area ("more settings") with additional user-configurable options accessible after login.
Add a background image/visual layer to the sign-in page (use the provided asset as the login background).
Support per-month background images (allow different background images to be used depending on the current month).
Adjust typography styling so fonts/letters appear brighter (improve text color/contrast/legibility across the UI).
